description: Writing a field of a binding in a terminal substate fires E-STATE-TERMINAL-MUTATION (§54.6.4). `done` is typed `Validated`, a terminal substate (zero declared outgoing transitions); `done.body = "tampered"` mutates a resting state's field, which has no defined semantics, so the type system reports E-STATE-TERMINAL-MUTATION. The canonical `< Validated body(string)>` substate syntax incidentally co-fires E-TYPE-UNKNOWN-NAME + W-WHITESPACE-001, left out of notCodes; W-PROGRAM-SPA-INFERRED is ignored by superset matching.
rationale: §54.6.4 a terminal substate (no outgoing transitions) is a resting state; writing any of its fields is a compile error (E-STATE-TERMINAL-MUTATION), steering to either declaring a transition or reconsidering the life-cycle design.
